_comment: Oracle scenarios for cross-checking our engine against lobogrande's source-faithful Python engine. Zero upgrades and zero cards, so the per-point base math is compared with no upgrade-index mapping needed. base_damage_const=10 and base stamina=100 match their hardcoded defaults; base crit-damage 1.5x and base super-crit mult 2.0x match their bases. build is S/A/P/I/L/D/C attribute points.
_upgradeComment: Per-point skill-buff upgrades. Each maps a single our-field to a single their-row (verified): modStrA0->25, modStrA1->47, modAgi->26, modPer->33, modInt->35, modDivA2->34, modCorrA2->52. Both engines get the same level; we then compare the same derived stats.
_coeffComment: The damage%/armor-pen% pool upgrades are folded into the displayed base in our model (reverseBaseStats divides them back out), so they cannot be set on a clean base without double-applying. They join the SAME pool as the per-point strDamagePct / intArmorPen that the stat scenarios already validate, so the only thing left to verify is that our per-level coefficient equals their UPGRADE_DEF value. row gives their UPGRADE_DEF F-multiplier; incField is the our-side coefficient one level adds.
_cardComment: Card HP/reward multipliers by rarity. Their get_card_bonuses(level) vs our cardBonusForState(state). Levels: 1 standard, 2 gilded, 3 polychrome; F41 (row 41) is the +15% polychrome upgrade.
_spawnComment: Block-spawn model: confirm our per-slot rarity rate table equals their actual sampled spawn distribution (their 24-slot sequential 1-in-X rolls), and surface the active-node count difference from our 6-node-per-floor minimum.
_runComment: Phase 2: run RESULTS. Run a full dig many times in both engines (zero upgrades/cards, no Luck/Div so there are no stamina refunds, crosshairs, or auto-abilities) and compare the average floor reached. Their micro-tick sim and our EV+Monte-Carlo model agree in expectation, so floor is gated within a tolerance and block count is informational. Builds use only S/A/P/I to keep the dig deterministic in its mechanics.
_blockComment: Block HP/armor check across the deep-floor scaling, including the floor-150 armor-skip and floor-300 double-trigger bugs lobogrande's engine preserves. Zero cards. Their block ids are <type><tier>; we map type com->common, leg->legendary, myth->mythic, div->divine.
